mnemonics.title: Remember Cyclomatic Number Formula
mnemonics.content: My Naughty Children Play - M = N + C - P becomes m-n+c. Think: m (edges) minus n (vertices) plus c (components). M = m - n + c (cyclomatic number = edges minus vertices plus components)

acronyms.usage: Matrix representing fundamental circuits in a graph with respect to a spanning tree
Binary field used for circuit matrix operations where 1+1=0
Algorithm to find spanning tree for constructing fundamental circuits

visualTips.visualization: Draw a tree with bold edges, add one dotted chord edge - the unique path between chord endpoints plus the chord itself forms a fundamental circuit (looks like one loop)
Think of F-matrix as a table: rows = fundamental circuits (loops), columns = edges. Mark 1 where edge is in that circuit, 0 otherwise. Creates a pattern like a musical staff.
Circuit and Cut-set matrices are like perpendicular vectors - their dot product (mod 2) equals zero

analogies.analogy: Like adding one new road (chord) to an existing road network (tree) - the new road + existing roads to get back creates one new route/circuit
Think of it as the number of 'extra' roads beyond the minimum needed to connect all cities. Minimum = n-1 (tree), extras = m - (n-1)
Imagine all possible loops you can trace in a road network. Each loop is a vector in this space, and you can combine them (XOR) to get other loops.
